fitia.subtitle: AI-powered fitness coaching application — personalised training programmes generated via RAG and LLM.
fitia.context1: FitIA was born from a simple observation: existing fitness coaching apps offer generic programmes that are poorly tailored to individual needs. The goal was to build a system capable of generating truly personalised training programmes by leveraging the power of LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation.
fitia.context2: [Fill in here with your project context — why you launched FitIA, in what framework, what the initial constraints were…]
fitia.archIntro: The FitIA pipeline relies on a multi-step RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture:
fitia.arch1: The user enters their goals, level, and constraints via the interface.
fitia.arch2: Data is encoded and a similarity search is performed in Weaviate to retrieve the most relevant exercises.
fitia.arch3: The enriched context is passed to the LLM (via OpenRouter) to generate a structured programme.
fitia.arch4: The response is formatted and returned via the FastAPI.
fitia.arch5: [Fill in with the specific details of your implementation…]
fitia.result1: [Key result 1 — e.g.: RAG accuracy, programme quality…]
fitia.result2: [Key result 2 — e.g.: scalability, performance, user feedback…]
fitia.result3: [Key result 3 — e.g.: what you learned, challenges overcome…]

llm.subtitle: Fine-tuning LLMs for automatic analysis of Wireshark network traces — project carried out during my apprenticeship at Orange.
llm.context1: During my apprenticeship at Orange (Lannion), I worked on leveraging language models to automatically analyse network captures. Wireshark traces represent massive volumes of data that are difficult to interpret manually — the goal was to automate this analysis using LLMs.
llm.context2: [Fill in here with the specific context — team, business stakes, any confidentiality constraints…]
llm.approachIntro: The approach was structured in several phases:
llm.approach1: Extraction and preprocessing of network traces from PCAP files (tshark → structured JSON).
llm.approach2: Building a training dataset from annotated traces.
llm.approach3: Fine-tuning a base LLM via LoRA/QLoRA to specialise the model for telecom network analysis.
llm.approach4: Evaluating the performance of the fine-tuned model vs. the base model on analysis tasks.
llm.result1: [Key result 1 — e.g.: benchmark score, accuracy gain…]
llm.result2: [Key result 2 — e.g.: reduced analysis time, validated use cases…]
llm.result3: [Key result 3 — e.g.: identified limitations, future directions…]

motowest.title: MotoWest
motowest.subtitle: Full-stack e-commerce platform for a Breton motorcycle parts retailer — interactive catalogue, exploded diagrams, used parts marketplace and integrated business management.
motowest.context: MotoWest is a motorcycle parts and accessories retailer based in Concarneau (Brittany), specialising in the SHERCO brand. The client needed a digital solution covering all their operations: new parts sales, used parts resale, stock management and order processing. I designed and developed a custom Django platform end-to-end, deployed in production on Render with a PostgreSQL database.
motowest.arch1: Dual catalogue: separate management of new parts (MotoPiece) and used parts (OccasionPiece), with product sheets, photos and real-time stock tracking.
motowest.arch2: Interactive exploded diagrams: motorcycle parts schematics visualisation, allowing users to identify and order a part directly from the diagram.
motowest.arch3: Stripe checkout: full payment integration with webhook handling, order confirmation and automatic emails via Gmail SMTP.
motowest.arch4: Django Admin back-office: custom order, stock, user and permission management interface tailored for the client.
motowest.arch5: GDPR compliance: cookie consent, legal notices, privacy policy and personal data management built in from the ground up.
motowest.arch6: Responsive design: mobile and desktop interface with the Audiowide font and MotoWest's blue/yellow brand identity.
motowest.result1: Complete platform delivered end-to-end, covering catalogue, payment, stock management and legal compliance for a real client in production.
motowest.result2: Scalable Django architecture allowing new brands, categories and features to be added without rearchitecting the core application.
motowest.result3: Proactive GDPR compliance implemented before deploying tracking tools, avoiding costly after-the-fact remediation.
motowest.result4: Integrated professional invoicing system meeting French auto-entrepreneur legal requirements and the client's management needs.
